text: The ability of large language models (LLMs) to represent geometric concepts and spatial relationships has not yet been well explored. In this research, we leveraged the capabilities of two LLMs, namely GPT-2 and BERT, to discover the representational capabilities of such models for geometric data and relationships. We used different architectures of classifiers and regressors to test the abilities of the embeddings obtained from the input of Well-Known Text (WKT) descriptions of geometries. Our experiments showed that the embeddings are able to well preserve the properties of the input geometric data up to the level of accuracy of 73% and represent some spatial relationships. However, many aspects including regression of exact numerical values and spatial search of geometric data related to query data remain to be addressed by GeoAI in the coming future, which calls for better knowledge incorporation.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
